name: Soybean Curd (Tofu)
recipeInstructions:
1. Rinse the soybeans thoroughly in plenty of water. Place them in a large glass jar and cover with enough water to fully submerge them. Allow to soak for at least 12 hours.
2. Drain the soybeans again in plenty of water.
3. Blend the soaked soybeans with 2 quarts of water using a food processor or blender until a smooth paste is formed.
4. Line a fine-mesh strainer with damp muslin cloth and strain the mixture through it, pressing to extract as much liquid as possible.
5. Slowly bring the strained liquid to a gentle boil in a large pot over medium heat, stirring constantly. Cook for 10 minutes.
6. In a small bowl, dissolve calcium sulphate in 3/4 cup cold water using a whisk or fork. Line another fine-mesh strainer with damp muslin cloth and strain through it.
7. Add the dissolved calcium sulphate to the soybean mixture and stir well.
8. Pour the mixture into a large mold lined with wet muslin.
9. Cover the surface of the curd with wet muslin.
10. Place a board or plate weighted with an object weighing no more than 11 lbs on top. Allow to set for up to 40 minutes, then carefully cut the tofu into pieces and wrap in foil.
11. Store in a large jar covered with cold water.
